name: Vor
description: Vor is a Norse goddess associated with wisdom, knowledge, and awareness. She is one of the Asynjur, the principal goddesses of the Norse pantheon. Vor is known for her keen observation skills and her ability to perceive everything that transpires in the world. Nothing is hidden from her, and she possesses a deep understanding of events, both past and present. Her name, meaning 'careful' or 'aware', perfectly reflects her role as an all-seeing and all-knowing figure. While not a prominent character in surviving Norse myths in the same way as figures like Odin or Thor, Vor's presence underscores the importance of knowledge and awareness within Norse cosmology. She is believed to reside in Asgard and may have played a role in advising other gods and goddesses with her unparalleled insight. Some scholars suggest she might have been a lesser-known but significant figure in daily life, invoked for guidance and understanding. Her connection to oaths and agreements is also suggested, as her awareness would make her a fitting witness to such important events. Vor embodies the Norse value of careful observation and the pursuit of wisdom, reflecting the importance of understanding the world and its events.
subtitle: The All-Seeing, All-Knowing Goddess

keyMyths.title: Vor's Observation of Loki
keyMyths.description: Vor witnessed Loki's mischievous behavior, providing insight to the other gods. This led to a better understanding of Loki's true nature and motives.

keyMyths.title: Vor and the Sacred Oaths
keyMyths.description: Vor is believed to be a witness to sacred oaths, ensuring their adherence. Her keen awareness makes her a powerful guardian of truth and justice.

keyMyths.title: Vor's Insightful Advice
keyMyths.description: Vor is consulted for her advice, providing valuable insights to the other deities. Her wisdom helps them navigate complex situations and make informed decisions.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
